channel.title: U.S. Police Shooting Research Tracker
channel.description: Recent academic research on police shootings and use of deadly force

item.title: Racial and ethnic disparities in fatal police shootings: Variation across U.S. states and the role of firearm ownership.
item.description:
Authors: Neil R, Bushway S, Schell TL et al.
Journal: PLoS One

item.title: Contesting repression in divided democracies: Can counternarratives reduce support for police violence?
item.description:
Authors: González & Skigin
Journal: American Journal of Political Science

Abstract Democracies often struggle to contain police violence, which undermines the rule of law and reproduces inequality by targeting marginalized citizens. Yet, scholars have shown that repressive policing enjoys considerable public support. Focusing on Brazil, we demonstrate that attitudes toward police violence mirror polarization between left‐ and right‐wing voters, and investigate how competing narratives change attitudes toward police vi...

item.title: Victim-blaming, tunnel vision, and the “hero woman officer”: the portrayal of police in American Nightmare (2024) and Unbelievable (2019)
item.description:
Authors: Slakoff & Montano Ayon
Journal: Policing: An International Journal

Purpose This study examines how policing is framed within true crime television, with particular attention to narratives involving violence against women and victims whose credibility is questioned by law enforcement. Design/methodology/approach Using thematic analysis, the ...
